parameters.content:
## BakaClipz Clip Auto-Generator

**Flow:**
1. Every 6h → pull **currently live top streams** from Twitch (sorted by viewers)
2. Filter by MIN_LIVE_VIEWERS, cap at MAX_STREAMERS_TO_CHECK
3. Fetch clips from past 24h for each → score by view-velocity + duration sweet-spot
4. Dedupe vs Supabase `processed_clips`
5. JSON2Video → transcribe + burn viral captions + 9:16 reformat
6. Poll until done → download MP4 → upload to Google Drive
7. Log to Supabase

**Discovery is dynamic now** — no static streamer list to maintain.
Whoever is live & popular at run-time gets clipped.

**Auth:**
- Twitch HTTP nodes use **BakaClipz Twitch Credential** (Generic OAuth2, Client Credentials)
- `Client-Id` header sent manually from Config

**Config knobs:**
- MIN_LIVE_VIEWERS — lower bar = wider net (1000 is a good start)
- MAX_STREAMERS_TO_CHECK — caps clips API calls (25 = 25 requests/run)
- STREAMS_LANGUAGE — 'en', 'es', 'ja' etc. Leave empty for global
- TOP_N_PER_STREAMER — clips kept per streamer after virality scoring

### Polling loop
Wait → Get Status → IF done?
- Yes → Download → Drive → Log
- No → Poll Guard (caps at 10 tries ~5 min) → back to Wait

JSON2Video typical render time for ≤60s clips: 20–90s.

### Supabase schema
```sql
create table processed_clips (
  clip_id text primary key,
  broadcaster_login text,
  title text,
  view_count int,
  virality_score numeric,
  drive_file_id text,
  rendered_url text,
  created_at timestamptz default now()
);
create index on processed_clips (broadcaster_login, created_at desc);
```

### MP4 URL resolution

Twitch's Helix API doesn't expose direct MP4 URLs.

The old hack — derive from `thumbnail_url` by stripping `-preview-WxH.jpg` and appending `.mp4` — only worked for clips on Twitch's legacy CDN.

New clips are stored on `static-cdn.jtvnw.net/twitch-vap-video-assets-prod-*` and have no direct thumbnail→mp4 mapping.

This node calls Twitch's **undocumented GraphQL** (same endpoint their own site + every clip downloader uses) to get the signed source URL.

- Endpoint: `gql.twitch.tv/gql`
- Client-Id: `kimne78kx3ncx6brgo4mv6wki5h1ko` (public web client)
- Persisted query: `VideoAccessToken_Clip`
- Returns signed URL valid ~24h

### Supabase schema additions
```sql
alter table processed_clips
  add column if not exists ai_virality_score int,
  add column if not exists ai_audience_fit text,
  add column if not exists ai_emotion_type text,
  add column if not exists ai_hook_strength int,
  add column if not exists ai_reasoning text,
  add column if not exists ai_recommended_for_publish boolean,
  add column if not exists captions jsonb,
  add column if not exists ready_for_publish boolean default false,
  add column if not exists ai_analyzed_at timestamptz;
```

### Gemini prompt notes

- Single call returns both virality analysis AND captions per platform.
- `responseMimeType: application/json` forces structured output (no markdown fences).
- Temperature 0.7 — enough variance for caption creativity, not so much that JSON breaks.

**Model choice (May 2026):**
- `gemini-3.5-flash` (default) — GA as of May 19, 2026. Frontier-tier intelligence at Flash speed, ~$1.50/$9 per 1M tokens. Best video understanding in Flash tier.
- `gemini-3-flash` — still solid, ~3× cheaper ($0.50/$3 per 1M). Use if cost matters more than the last 10% of quality.
- `gemini-3.5-pro` — not GA yet (slated next month per Google). Swap in when it lands for sharpest cultural read.

**If captions feel mid:**
Edit the prompt in `Gemini: Analyze + Caption`. Give it 2-3 examples of your own best-performing captions (few-shot) and quality jumps noticeably.

## BakaClipz - Virality Filter + Caption Generator

**Trigger:** new .mp4 lands in your clips folder (from the main BakaClipz workflow).

**Flow:**
1. Drive trigger detects new file
2. Parse `{broadcaster_login}_{clip_id}.mp4` from filename
3. Look up clip metadata in Supabase (title, view count, etc.)
4. Download the MP4 → base64
5. Single Gemini call: virality analysis + captions for TikTok / IG Reels / YT Shorts / X
6. Write everything back to Supabase
7. If virality score ≥ PUBLISH_THRESHOLD and `recommended_for_publish` is true → mark `ready_for_publish=true`

**Setup:**
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` in n8n env
- Supabase schema additions (see schema sticky)
- Drive folder same as main workflow's `DRIVE_FOLDER_ID`

**Knobs in Config:**
- PUBLISH_THRESHOLD (default 7) — min virality score to flag for publish
- GEMINI_MODEL (default `gemini-2.5-flash`) — swap to `gemini-2.5-pro` for sharper cultural take, ~5× cost

### Notes on the upload-post payload

**TikTok + Instagram caption handling:**
Their docs explicitly say the global `description` field is IGNORED for these platforms. So we concatenate `caption + hashtags` into `tiktok_title` and `instagram_title`.

**YouTube hashtags:**
Inlined in `youtube_description`. YouTube auto-detects #tags in description and displays the first three above the title.

**X URL stripping:**
Upload-Post automatically strips any URLs from X posts (saves 13× on X API cost). Our prompt doesn't generate URLs anyway, but worth knowing.

**Async fallback:**
If the upload takes >59s, upload-post switches to background and returns a `request_id`. We store it for later status polling via GET `/api/uploadposts/status?request_id=...`.

## BakaClipz - Publish to Socials

**Trigger:** Supabase database webhook fires when a row in `processed_clips` is updated.

**Flow:**
1. Webhook receives Supabase payload
2. Filter: only proceed if `ready_for_publish=true` AND not already posted
3. Build per-platform captions from `captions` JSONB (TikTok and IG ignore `description`, so we put caption + hashtags into `tiktok_title` / `instagram_title`)
4. POST to Upload-Post API with all 4 platforms (TikTok, IG, YouTube, X)
5. Supabase: mark `posted_to_socials=true`, store the response

**Supabase webhook setup:**
Dashboard → Database → Webhooks → Create new
- Source: `processed_clips`
- Events: UPDATE
- HTTP method: POST
- URL: this workflow's webhook URL (visible on the Webhook node after activation)

**Idempotency:**
Upload-Post `Idempotency-Key` header set to `clip_id` so retries don't double-post.

parameters.query:
select clip_id, broadcaster_login, title, view_count, virality_score,
       drive_file_id
from processed_clips
where drive_file_id is not null
  and ai_analyzed_at is null
order by created_at asc;

record.title: 2 hours of hard work

tiktok.caption: not @Ludwig spending 2 hours in FL studio just to make a frantic nintendo track 😭

x_twitter.post: the instant realization that you cooked absolute garbage after 2 hours in FL Studio is too real (via @Ludwig)

youtube_shorts.title: Ludwig's 2-hour FL Studio beat made him regret everything 💀
youtube_shorts.description:
Ludwig spends two hours cooking in FL studio only to immediately regret his life choices.

#shorts #ludwig #producer

instagram_reels.caption: The immediate regret of listening back to a beat you spent 2 hours cooking. @ludwig was not happy with his FL Studio creation 💀

record.ai_reasoning: The instant contrast between two hours of hard work and immediate, aggressive self-hatred is highly relatable to anyone who does creative work.

body.message: You have reached the daily posting cap on youtube. Used 10/10 in the last 24 hours. Consider scheduling for later.
